stories.title: Tarik Skubal — First Win in Two Months, Trade Deadline Coming Fast

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal just had back-to-back starts for the ages.

June 29 vs Minnesota: 7 IP, 1 H, 0 ER, 13 strikeouts.
June 30 vs New York: 6 IP, 1 H, 1 ER, 9 strikeouts.

His first win since April 18. The deadline is in five weeks.

#MLB #Tigers #BallparkBanter

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Skubal's trade value has never been higher.

Two consecutive 1-hit outings. 22 combined strikeouts in three days.

The Dodgers are the frontrunner. The deadline is August 3.

Tigers are sellers. One of the best pitchers on the planet is being moved.

#MLB #BallparkBanter #Tigers

facebook.long_form:
Tarik Skubal missed over a month with an elbow injury, came back and struggled through four rocky starts, and then — in the last three days of June — looked like the best pitcher on the planet.

June 29 against the Twins: 7 innings, one hit, zero runs, 13 strikeouts. June 30 against the Yankees at Yankee Stadium: 6 innings, one hit, one earned run, 9 strikeouts. Twenty-two strikeouts in two consecutive starts spanning three days. His first win since April 18. The Tigers won both games.

The trade deadline is August 3. Skubal is a pending free agent. ESPN's Jeff Passan has pegged the trade probability at 85%. The Dodgers are reported as the frontrunner. When teams around the league were calling Detroit in May and June, Skubal was dealing with an elbow procedure and a slow return. The calls are being made again now — with very different context on the other end.

A player coming off the most dominant back-to-back stretch of his career, who won the 2025 AL Cy Young Award with a 2.21 ERA and 241 strikeouts, is going to be traded in approximately five weeks. The market for him has never been clearer.

[Posting Window: 9:00 AM ET]

article.headline: Skubal's First Win in Two Months Comes With a Trade Deadline Five Weeks Away

stories.title: Jacob deGrom — Back. Fully Back. July 1 Against Cleveland.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Jacob deGrom today: 7 innings, 2 earned runs, Rangers win 4-2.

He went 4-0 in June. The Rangers have won 6 in a row.

The return is not a partial comeback. It is complete.

#MLB #Rangers #BallparkBanter

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Jacob deGrom missed most of two seasons with elbow and back injuries.

Today he threw 7 innings against the Cleveland Guardians and the Rangers won.

The comeback story of this baseball season has a really good ending so far.

#MLB #BallparkBanter #Rangers

facebook.long_form:
Jacob deGrom has been baseball's great comeback story for three years, repeatedly teasing a full return before another injury sent him back to the injured list. Today — July 1, 2026 — feels like the culmination.

deGrom threw 7 innings against the Cleveland Guardians. He allowed 2 earned runs on a Jose Manzardo two-run home run. The Rangers won 4-2. Texas has now won 6 consecutive games. deGrom went 4-0 during the month of June. That is four consecutive decisions with zero losses in June, across seven-inning outings, for a pitcher who missed significant stretches of the 2023 and 2024 seasons.

This is no longer a partial comeback. The questions about whether deGrom could sustain anything resembling his old form have been answered by the calendar. He has been healthy and effective for an extended run. The Texas Rangers are competing in the AL West with him at the top of their rotation, and he is earning it.

[Posting Window: 11:00 AM ET]

article.headline: Jacob deGrom Threw 7 Innings Today. The Return Is Complete.

stories.title: Kyle Schwarber — 30 Home Runs, and the Derby Is at His House

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 30 home runs.

The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — his home stadium.

He has not said yes.

#MLB #Phillies #BallparkBanter

facebook.long_form:
Kyle Schwarber is the first player in baseball to hit 30 home runs in 2026. He did it on June 28. He leads the major leagues. He plays for the Philadelphia Phillies. And on July 13, Citizens Bank Park — his home stadium — will host the Home Run Derby.

He has not committed to participating.

That last sentence is what makes this interesting. The Home Run Derby at a player's own ballpark, while that player leads the major leagues in home runs, is about as obvious a story as the All-Star Week calendar can produce. And yet Schwarber has kept the decision open. Whether that is strategic, logistical, superstitious, or simply undecided is not public knowledge. What is public is that every day that passes without a yes from the most prolific home run hitter in baseball, while a countdown clock ticks toward a Derby at his own park, is a day this story gets more attention.

[Posting Window: 1:00 PM ET]

article.headline: Schwarber Hit 30 Home Runs First. The Derby Is at His Park. He Hasn't Committed.

stories.title: All-Star Phase 2 Closes Tomorrow — What's Left to Decide

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
All-Star Phase 2 voting closes July 2 at noon ET.

That is tomorrow.

Cam Schlittler is your AL starting pitcher. Rosters get announced July 4 on FOX. The Home Run Derby is July 13 on Netflix.

Last chance to vote. #MLB #AllStarGame #BallparkBanter

facebook.long_form:
The calendar is running out on All-Star Phase 2 voting. The polls close July 2 at noon Eastern — that is tomorrow, and the window tonight is the last real opportunity for fans to push their candidates over the line.

Here is where things stand heading into the final hours: Cam Schlittler has been announced as the American League starting pitcher, which makes him the first rookie to start the Midsummer Classic as a pitcher in recent memory. Full rosters will be announced July 4 on FOX. The Home Run Derby on July 13 will air on Netflix — a first for the event.

The rest of the field is being finalized now, in real time, based on the Phase 2 vote totals. If there is a player you want to see in the starting lineup on July 15 at Citizens Bank Park, tonight is the last chance to vote.

[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

stories.title: Jacob Misiorowski — The Case for a First-Year Cy Young

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Jacob Misiorowski, Brewers:

9-3. 1.45 ERA. 0.768 WHIP. 146 strikeouts — leads all of MLB.

He threw 105.5 mph on June 26.

Milwaukee is 52-31, the best record in the National League.

The Cy Young argument is real. #MLB #Brewers #BallparkBanter

facebook.long_form:
The conversation about the 2026 National League Cy Young Award has to include Jacob Misiorowski, and it has to take him seriously.

The Milwaukee Brewers right-hander is 9-3 with a 1.45 ERA, a 0.768 WHIP, and 146 strikeouts — which leads all of Major League Baseball. On June 26, he threw a pitch measured at 105.5 miles per hour, one of the fastest pitches ever recorded in MLB history. The Brewers are 52-31, the best record in the National League.

The historical precedent for a first-year pitcher winning the Cy Young Award is thin. The award has almost always gone to established veterans with a track record of dominance. But the argument for precedent giving way to performance gets considerably more compelling when the candidate leads the league in strikeouts, has a sub-1.50 ERA in the second half of the season, and plays for the team with the NL's best record. Misiorowski is not making an argument by reputation. He is making it with the numbers, and the numbers are this good.

[Posting Window: 5:30 PM ET]

article.headline: Jacob Misiorowski Is Leading the League in Strikeouts and Making a Cy Young Case in His First Full Season
